Several haw's showing lots of immature fruit in july, also spotted an apple mixed in/ Falling Fruit season: September-November.
Decent-sized crabapples, tree furthest in has largest fruits, approximately quarter-sized.
Plentiful clover in mowed area with some plantain and dandelion. Many of the small trees in mowed area are crabapples that produce small fruits.
Some type of apple tree, probably crabapple. Another tree is east of here along brush edge at top of hill.
One crabapple across wooden foot bridge and a bit to the south.

Various crabapples (possible apples?) Flowering scattered all around the buildings and wood edges in this business park area. (just placing one point for whole area currently) Falling Fruit season: September-November.

Plants spotted between the picnic area and along sugarbush trail down to the stream. There's also quite a bit of Ironwood. Beware the poison ivy, the trails are narrow and long pants would be recommended to protect against it.
Several trees, mostly young scattered through wooded area.
Many large mature trees around small parking area, black walnuts showing nuts as of fall 2021. Falling Fruit season: September-October.

Many mature black walnut trees around the northern parking area and other nearby areas. Showing nuts as of fall 2021. Falling Fruit season: September-November.

Heavy with nuts as of 2017, east of entrance to southern parking area.
Towards north end of patch, lots of unripe nuts visible. Falling Fruit season: August-October.
Trio of mature tall trees near stream/street crossing, still in mowed area. Showing nuts as of 2017.
A few younger trees present, old enough to make nuts. There's an older walnut further in past the giant white oak.
Saw nuts on tree near intersection. There's more trees around park parking area that may be walnuts as well but cannot 100% ID them due to height.
Patch of berrybushes right next to path, mostly in afternoon shade. Almost all berries are gone as of 7/25/16. Falling Fruit season: July-July.
Many Blackberries all along brushy property edge running east-west here.
Blackberry and grape both abundant along eastern edge and showing unripe fruits. Falling Fruit season: July-August.
Can be hard to see plants through all the goldenrod.
Blackberry bushes along edge of mowed area. Falling Fruit season: July-August.

Blackberry along the wood edge in lesser mowed area.
Grape with fruit showing - be careful not to confuse with the non-edible virginia creeper next to it.
Many wild rose and grapes near dead trees. Look like small hips on the rose. Other rose scattered through brighter areas in woods.
Several grape vines throughout wooded area, some displaying fruit. Most fruit is out of arms reach.
Some grape vines climbing on other shrubs in brushy line, not currently flowering or fruiting.
Blackberry and grape both abundant along eastern edge and showing unripe fruits. Falling Fruit season: July-August.
Mayapple dotted in patches, looks like some type of grape vine scattered along this edge of woods/parking lot as well as some type of rose bush. Falling Fruit season: May-June.
Some grapes displaying unripe fruit as of 7/3/16. Falling Fruit season: July-August.
At least one kind of between the two parking lots and fence area. Falling Fruit season: August-November.
Many shagbark hickories ranging from saplings up to giant mature trees. Dropping nuts as of 2021. Falling Fruit season: September-November.
Some young shagbarks in this little strip of trees, mostly along western edge. One located at marker point at parking lot corner. Falling Fruit season: September-November.
Scattered along both sides of pathway, some with fruit.
Many shrubby trees scattered about field. (fruits all gone by end of sept) Falling Fruit season: August-September.
More srubby trees near water overflow for lake. Also some along edge of woods.
Shrubby tree near parking area - showing small immature fruits currently. (fruits all gone by end of sept) Falling Fruit season: August-September.
Plenty on slope north of fence down towards parking lot, all along length. Falling Fruit season: September-September.
Both Tall and Grass-leaf varieties all over field area. Falling Fruit season: August-September.
Lots of goldenrod in unmowed meadow area.
Lots of Goldenrod & a good amount of queen ann's lace in unmowed area - beware of the poison ivy growing there as well!
Lots of Goldenrod in this field. Beware, lots of poison ivy as well.
Tall goldenrod north of building in this field area.
Many mature flowering shrubs, also some honeysuckle vines. All scattered all around this area especially on wood edges.

Plants spotted between the picnic area and along sugarbush trail down to the stream. There's also quite a bit of Ironwood. Beware the poison ivy, the trails are narrow and long pants would be recommended to protect against it.
Several kinds of oaks, lots along edge of woods, also more scattered around park.
Largest tree here is a white oak. Most of the rest of the oaks are red. Falling Fruit season: September-November.
Largest tree here is a white oak. Most of the rest of the oaks are red. Falling Fruit season: September-November.
Some types of oak, large mature trees.
Scattered in woods around trail. Several different kinds of cherry, though none look reachable for harvesting. Beechs, oaks and cherries seem most dominant.
Mostly black locust, a couple oaks and at least one haw along brush edge south of building.
More red oaks than white, scattered in wood area.
More red oaks than white, scattered in wood area.
Small tree in little thicket area, showing some fruits. There's a few larger trees around park as well.
Spotted some young trees along wood edge, too young to fruit. May be more mature trees inside woods though.
About 3-4 pear trees at wood edge near road. Not a lot of fruit showing though.
Near wood edge, some wild strawberry plants.
Narrowleaf Plantain, wild strawberry, and what is possibly scotch broom all scattered in drier, less fertile area near bottom of incline towards building.
